hero.tagline: MCP-first · open source · EVM + Solana aware
hero.title: Less Dashboard, More Dialogue: Error Tracking, Designed for Agents
hero.subtitle: Read. Eval. Triage. Loop. — every issue, event, and breadcrumb is one MCP tool-call away. Arguslog runs as your agent's debugging surface, so triage happens in the same chat where you're already coding — no tab-switching, no filter dropdowns. Self-hostable and multi-tenant, with first-class SDKs across JS, JVM, Python, and a Web3 add-on for viem / ethers / Solana / Anchor.

features.heading: Everything you need, nothing you don't.
features.subheading: Built on the same multi-tenant stack we run in production. The wire format, DSN scheme, and SDK shape are all Arguslog-native — no vendor lock-in to inherit, no bridge layer to maintain.

ingest.title: Real-time ingest
ingest.body: Events flow through a Redis Streams queue with at-least-once delivery semantics. Issues are deduplicated by stable fingerprints in milliseconds.

sourcemaps.title: Source-mapped stack traces
sourcemaps.body: Upload source maps per release through the CLI. Frames are symbolicated by the worker so your team sees real file paths, not minified gibberish.

breadcrumbs.title: Auto-breadcrumb timeline
breadcrumbs.body: Browser SDK auto-instruments console, fetch, XHR, navigation, clicks, web vitals, long tasks, and worker errors. Every issue lands with the lead-up rendered as a coloured timeline so the cause reads at a glance.

web3.title: Web3 — first-class
web3.body: viem, ethers v6, @solana/web3.js, Anchor, wagmi, and WalletConnect errors decoded into rich tags: chain, wallet, contract, function, args, decoded revert reason / Anchor error / custom program code. One initWeb3() wires the lot.

alerts.title: Multi-channel alerts
alerts.body: Telegram, Slack, email, and generic webhooks out of the box. Per-rule throttling and DSL-driven matching on level, tags, and first-seen windows.

slackInbound.title: Triage in Slack
slackInbound.body: Install the Arguslog Slack app and run `/arguslog issues|issue <id>|resolve <id>|release <version>|set-project <slug>` from any channel. Mutations broadcast in-channel so the team sees who fixed what without a separate ping.

team.title: Team workflows
team.body: Owner / admin / member roles, invite flows with email verification, scoped Personal Access Tokens for CI and scripts, full RFC 9457 problem responses.

platforms.heading: First-class SDKs
platforms.subheading: Drop-in clients for browser, mobile, and server runtimes — including a zero-dependency Python SDK, a Spring Boot autoconfig, and a Web3 add-on you layer on top of any JS SDK. The catalog below is fetched live from /api/v1/platforms, the same endpoint the dashboard uses.
platforms.fallback: Visit the GitHub repo for the SDK list.
platforms.cardAria: Open the {{name}} SDK README on GitHub (new tab)

web3Card.title: Web3 add-on
web3Card.description: EVM (viem + ethers + wagmi + WalletConnect) and Solana (web3.js + Anchor + wallet adapter) error decoding. Layers on top of @arguslog/sdk-browser.
web3Card.aria: Open the Web3 SDK README on GitHub (new tab)

mcp.heading: MCP-first by design
mcp.subheading: "Designed for agents" isn't a label — the MCP surface is the product, not a side-channel. Wire @arguslog/mcp-server into Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, or Continue and your agent gets authenticated, full-fidelity access to issues, projects, alerts, releases, and the platform admin surface, all gated by Personal Access Token scopes.
mcp.bullet1Body: Generate a Personal Access Token from the dashboard, drop a 4-line config into your MCP client, and your agent can ask questions like "what broke today?" or "open the latest production issue and walk me through the breadcrumb timeline". Stdio transport — no servers to host.
mcp.bullet2Title: Full API coverage out of the box
mcp.bullet2Body: Tools are auto-derived from the OpenAPI spec — every endpoint exposed in the dashboard is callable as a tool. The most-used operations have hand-curated descriptions and example payloads so the LLM picks the right tool first try.
coverage: Issues, events, breadcrumbs (the rich payload shape)
Slack workspace management (install / revoke / set default project)
Admin panel — tier grants, audit log (allowlisted PATs only)
mcp.configHint: Edit ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json (macOS) or the equivalent on your platform.

status.title: System status
status.subtitle: Real-time health of every public Arguslog service. Each tile probes the matching production endpoint from your browser on a 30-second loop — so what you see is exactly what your end users would see.
status.workerNote: The async worker (event fingerprinter / alert dispatcher) has no public port — it's monitored by Railway healthchecks against its internal /actuator/health. Outages there surface here indirectly: ingest still accepts events but issues won't update, so the API tile will report stale data instead.
status.incidentsEmpty: No incidents recorded. The log is kept in apps/landing/src/data/incidents.md; commits to main update this page.
